meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Students: Finish Your First Project
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Learn how to implement effective pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your project and launch with confidence.
content.answer_block: You fail to finish because you build features, not value. By defining a pricing model early, you force yourself to focus on the core 'must-have' functionality. Use Antigravity to scaffold your infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate on payment integration, effectively turning project scope creep into a profitable, shippable milestone.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with developers who spent months perfecting a login flow nobody asked for. You are currently trapped in the 'infinite build' loop, where your ambition exceeds your bandwidth, leading to inevitable burnout and a hollow portfolio. 

But what if you treated your code like a product from Day 1? By establishing a pricing structure—even for a student project—you shift your mindset from 'coding' to 'solving.' This pivot forces you to cut the fluff, prioritize the MVP, and finally ship something that validates your engineering potential.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the one action your user takes that justifies a fee. This is your 'North Star' for feature development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core user flow of my SaaS project. Suggest three pricing tiers based on usage volume vs. feature access. Keep the MVP scope extremely minimal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'What is the absolute minimum feature set required to justify a $9/mo tier?'

steps.title: Architect the Subscription Model
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold your database schema to support multi-tenancy and subscription states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema for a SaaS app with users, subscriptions, and usage_limits. Include a migration script for Stripe-ready fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a database migration for subscription_status and tier_level columns'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Payment Flow
steps.description: Implement the checkout session logic. Don't build custom billing; leverage Stripe's hosted checkout.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure server-side endpoint for Stripe Checkout sessions. Handle success and cancel webhooks.

steps.title: Implement Usage Gatekeeping
steps.description: Guard your core features behind a middleware that checks the user's subscription tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks if a user has sufficient credits or active subscription before allowing access to the core AI tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /refactor 'Apply the subscription-check middleware to all /api/generate routes'

steps.title: Finalize and Ship
steps.description: Clean up the code, remove debugging logs, and deploy your MVP for real user feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my project codebase for production readiness. Identify any hardcoded secrets or console logs that need removal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /lint 'Scan for security vulnerabilities and unused code before deployment'

pro_tips: Never build a custom billing system; use Stripe Checkout or Lemonsqueezy to avoid PCI compliance nightmares.
Charge $1.00 for your first user. The psychological shift of taking money makes you treat the product as a business, not a hobby.
If a feature doesn't contribute to your pricing tier's value, it's a distraction. Delete it.
Use usage-based pricing for AI apps to ensure your margins grow as your users' consumption scales.
Automate your feedback loop; if a user cancels, trigger an automated email asking exactly why.
content.next_steps: Deploy your app to Vercel or Railway, share the link in our community forum, and collect your first piece of user feedback within 24 hours.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in silence and start building for impact. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
